title: Welcome to Your Vault
content:
---
tags: home, welcome
---

# Welcome to Your Vault 👋

Take a deep breath and relax. This is your personal space to think, write, and connect your ideas. 

Whether you're writing code, drafting a novel, or organizing your thoughts, we've got you covered.

## Where to start?
If you're new here, we recommend checking out the [[/Formatting Guide/Basic Typography]] guide, or jumping straight into [[/Formatting Guide/Advanced Components]] if you want to see what this editor can really do.

Curious about how your notes connect? Open the [[/Knowledge Base/The Graph View]].

title: Basic Typography
content:
# Basic Typography

Writing should feel natural. Just start typing, and use simple symbols to format your text.

## Emphasis
Make your words stand out with **bold** text, *italics*, or ~~strikethrough~~. You can also ==highlight important phrases== to easily find them later.

## Lists
Organize your thoughts into clear lists:

### Unordered
- First, find a quiet place.
- Second, open a new note.
  - *Pro tip: use shortcuts!*

### Ordered
1. Write down your ideas.
2. Connect them using `[[links]]`.
3. Review your [[/Knowledge Base/The Graph View]].

> "The palest ink is better than the best memory."
> — Chinese Proverb

Ready for more? Check out [[/Formatting Guide/Advanced Components]].

title: Advanced Components
content:
# Advanced Components

When plain text isn't enough, we give you the tools to structure complex data.

## Tables
Keep your data neat and tidy:

| Feature | Status | Difficulty |
| :--- | :---: | ---: |
| Markdown | Active | Easy |
| Graph View | Beta | Medium |
| Syncing | Planning | Hard |

## Code Snippets
Are you a developer? Use inline code like `npm install`, or create full code blocks:

```typescript
function initializeVault() {
  console.log('Vault loaded!');
}
```

See how to make these stand out even more in [[/Formatting Guide/Custom Callouts]].

title: Custom Callouts
content:
---
tags: callouts, ui
---

# Custom Callouts

Callouts are a fantastic way to draw attention to specific parts of your notes.

> [!info] Did you know?
> You can nest callouts, lists, and even other markdown elements inside here.

> [!success] Goal Achieved
> You've successfully learned how to format text!

> [!warning] Careful
> Deleting a note cannot be undone unless you have a backup.

## To-Do Lists
You can also track your progress right inside your notes:
- [x] Read [[/Welcome to Your Vault]]
- [x] Learn about [[/Formatting Guide/Basic Typography]]
- [ ] Master [[/Formatting Guide/Advanced Components]]
- [ ] Explore [[/Knowledge Base/The Graph View]]

title: The Graph View
content:
# The Graph View

This is where the magic happens. Every time you use double brackets to link to another note, like [[/Welcome to Your Vault]], a connection is formed.

## Why use links?
By linking [[/Formatting Guide/Basic Typography]], [[/Formatting Guide/Advanced Components]], and [[/Formatting Guide/Custom Callouts]] together, you create a web of knowledge.

Over time, clusters will form around your most important topics, such as #productivity or #development. This mimics how your brain actually works!
